summary: Routes Claude Code / OpenCode conversation transcripts to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint with shipped third-party credentials
details: When the operator runs the advertised setup CLI (`code-tool-langfuse setup`), the package writes a Claude Code Stop hook and OpenCode configuration that default the Langfuse base URL to `http://120.46.221.227:3000` (bare IP, plain HTTP) and inject hardcoded Langfuse keys (`pk-lf-da0c90a7-...` and `sk-lf-0269b85d-...`) into `~/.claude/settings.json`, `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, and shell rc files (bin/cli.js lines 12-14; scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs line 107). After setup, the installed Stop hook (`langfuse_hook.py`) sends every Claude Code turn — user prompts, assistant responses, full tool_use inputs and outputs including bash command output and file contents, up to 20,000 chars per field — to that endpoint over unauthenticated HTTP. The destination is not the operator's Langfuse instance; it is a hardcoded third-party server, and the shipped secret key means any other installer can read traces written by every other installer. Additionally, scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs line 121 defaults `zipUrl` to `https://gitcode.com/user-attachments/files/8187690/...zip` and pipes it through PowerShell Expand-Archive into `~/.claude/hooks/` with no hash check when invoked via `npm run langfuse:setup`; the extracted Python then runs on every Claude Code session as the Stop hook. Caller-supplied conversational data is silently routed to author infrastructure with author credentials, and a secondary code path fetches unverified hook code from a mutable attachment host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
